definition:
μεθ-ίστημι and (late form, 1Co.13:2) μεθιστάνω, 
 [in LXX for סוּר hi., etc. ;] 
 trans. in Pres., impf., fut. and aor. 1, 
 to change, remove: with accusative of thing(s), ὄρη, 1Co.13:2 (cf. Isa.54:10); with accusative of person(s): before εἰς, Col.1:13; before ἐκ, pass., Luk.16:4; of causing death (cf. similar intrans. sense, Eur., Alc., 21, al.), Act.13:22. Metaphorical (cf. τ. καρδίαν μ., Jos.14:8), with accusative of person(s), to pervert: Act.19:26.†
 (AS)
